title: 【法治喪屍系列】今日判詞嘅陰濕位

replies.msg:
除左今日大家睇新聞入面嘅summary自外，其實今日法治喪屍再次自閹。

1. 法庭承認左殖民地政府對public danger and emergency 嘅用法 [133], [89]
包括：（1）戰爭狀態，（2）大規模破壞社會秩序，（3）疫情/天災，（4）金融災難，（5）任何其他嚴重災難

再加上係[132] 話得特首先有得定義public danger and emergency，所以姐係林鄭可以話「今日天氣有d凍」，之後就9立任何法例

2. 緊急法係無敵
本來緊急法立嘅法只係附屬法例，係大唔過立法會立嘅法，但係係[144]法庭就話係大過曬其他法。
同埋係[137]法庭又話緊急法係咁lun無敵架啦，因為好緊急啊嘛

邊個再話法治法庭有險可守，法庭都唔想咁嘅

replies.msg:
// 2. 緊急法係無敵
本來緊急法立嘅法只係附屬法例，係大唔過立法會立嘅法，但係係[144]法庭就話係大過曬其他法。//

補返原文
準確而言
上訴庭係認為「立法會通過緊急法，將修改任何法例嘅權力轉移畀特首」呢樣嘢無問題

E4.3 Section 2(2)(g)
This is the so-called “Henry‍ VIII Clause”:
“Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of subsection (1), such regulations may provide for– …
(g) amending any enactment, suspending the operation of any enactment and applying any enactment with or without modification;”

As Hogan‍ CJ observed in Li Bun, at p101, it is necessary to confer on the CE such power, otherwise her capability to make adequate provisions for some unexpected danger or emergency might be hampered or limited by the inability to alter any existing ordinance, especially, when the LegCo could not, for one reason or another, be brought into session or meet.

The Judges disagreed for two reasons.

At [64] of the Judgment, they first said that there is no provision in the BL that authorizes the CEIC by herself to amend or repeal primary legislation. That must be true. But the CEIC does not derive the power to amend or repeal primary legislation from the BL. She does so from the delegation by the LegCo under the common law. This leads to their second point.

At [65] - [66] of the Judgment, the Judges observed that validity of Henry ‍VIII Clauses is based on parliamentary sovereignty which the LegCo does not enjoy and that such Clauses are antithetical to the norm of subsidiary legislation as understood in Hong Kong under section ‍28(1)(b) of the Interpretation and General Clauses Ordinance (Cap‍ 1) (“the IGCO”) that “no subsidiary legislation shall be inconsistent with the provisions of any Ordinance”.

While it is true that the LegCo does not enjoy supremacy in the Diceyan sense, it is wrong to say that it cannot as a matter of Hong Kong common law delegate to the CE the power prescribed by section ‍2(2)(g) of the ERO in cases of emergency or public danger. There is nothing in the BL or anywhere else to indicate that such common law principle contravenes the BL and has not migrated to the post1997 era.

The Judges’ reliance on section‍ 28(1)(b) of IGCO is misplaced. Pursuant to section ‍2(1) of the same Ordinance, the application of the provisions of the IGCO is subject to the contrary intention that appears from the context of any ordinance. Given the context of the ERO, there is definitely a contrary intention to dis-apply section‍ 28(1)(b) to emergency regulations made thereunder.
狗官

上訴庭係認為「立法會通過緊急法，將修改任何法例嘅權力轉移畀特首」呢樣嘢無問題

E4.3 Section 2(2)(g)
This is the so-called “Henry‍ VIII Clause”:
“Without prejudice to the generality of the provisions of subsection (1), such regulations may provide for– …
(g) amending any enactment, suspending the operation of any enactment and applying any enactment with or without modification;”

As Hogan‍ CJ observed in Li Bun, at p101, it is necessary to confer on the CE such power, otherwise her capability to make adequate provisions for some unexpected danger or emergency might be hampered or limited by the inability to alter any existing ordinance, especially, when the LegCo could not, for one reason or another, be brought into session or meet.

The Judges disagreed for two reasons.

At [64] of the Judgment, they first said that there is no provision in the BL that authorizes the CEIC by herself to amend or repeal primary legislation. That must be true. But the CEIC does not derive the power to amend or repeal primary legislation from the BL. She does so from the delegation by the LegCo under the common law. This leads to their second point.

At [65] - [66] of the Judgment, the Judges observed that validity of Henry ‍VIII Clauses is based on parliamentary sovereignty which the LegCo does not enjoy and that such Clauses are antithetical to the norm of subsidiary legislation as understood in Hong Kong under section ‍28(1)(b) of the Interpretation and General Clauses Ordinance (Cap‍ 1) (“the IGCO”) that “no subsidiary legislation shall be inconsistent with the provisions of any Ordinance”.

While it is true that the LegCo does not enjoy supremacy in the Diceyan sense, it is wrong to say that it cannot as a matter of Hong Kong common law delegate to the CE the power prescribed by section ‍2(2)(g) of the ERO in cases of emergency or public danger. There is nothing in the BL or anywhere else to indicate that such common law principle contravenes the BL and has not migrated to the post1997 era.

The Judges’ reliance on section‍ 28(1)(b) of IGCO is misplaced. Pursuant to section ‍2(1) of the same Ordinance, the application of the provisions of the IGCO is subject to the contrary intention that appears from the context of any ordinance. Given the context of the ERO, there is definitely a contrary intention to dis-apply section‍ 28(1)(b) to emergency regulations made thereunder.

問題係立法會無權efface自己
依家ero係general legislative power

replies.msg:
無記錯的話
原訟庭嗰陣話根據基本法
立法權係屬於立法會，如果轉讓畀特首就係違反基本法下嘅權責劃分

但係依家上訴庭就引用《基本法》第五十六（二）
話緊急法符合基本法
// (e) ...《基本法》第五十六（二）條明確地認可，在普通法下行政長官可根據賦權法例制定緊急情況附屬法規。截至1997年7月1日爲止，《緊急條例》是唯一的賦權法例，可見《基本法》的草擬者當時顯然已預想了《緊急條例》及行政長官援引此條例制定緊急情況附屬法規的權力。他們必視《緊急條例》爲符合《基本法》，以確保《緊急條例》在1997年之後仍被保留爲香港特別行政區法律的一部分。根據以往援引《緊急條例》的經驗，《緊急條例》效力強大、概括面廣泛，足以讓行政長官能夠迅速和有效地立法，以應付對香港及香港市民構成嚴重和持續性威脅的所有和任何類型的緊急及危害公安情況。//

想請問下個判詞第144個度點解會係「法庭就話（緊急法立既法）係大過曬其他法」？以我理解個法官應該係想講因為IGCO第2條已經講到明"Save where the contrary intention appears either from this Ordinance or from the context of any other Ordinance or instrument"，所以ERO本身係唔受IGCO 28(1)(b)既限制，所以佢之後(第146段)先講個緊急法訂立之法例既penalties係違反IGCO 28(1)(e)都好，都唔會受IGCO限制，因為ERO係IGCO第2條黎講，佢本身就係預左會contrary intention appears either from this Ordinance or from the context of any other Ordinance or instrument

講到尾今次呢個法官講既野係法例上黎講係無錯，只不過佢太著重於法例本身，而無視而家香港呢個社會狀況，簡單Ｄ句即係離地，普通法應該係比civil law更加flexible，但今次呢個法官做唔到
